prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Think of the computer as a big brain, my dear grandchild, and the CPU is like the main worker bee in this brain, doing all the thinking and calculating. It stands for Central Processing Unit, which makes a lot of sense because it is like the boss of all the other parts. It's a tiny chip, really, but it's so important and works very hard. It performs calculations, follows instructions, and makes sure everything runs smoothly so you can play your games and surf the internet. You may not see it or hear it, but it is always busy making the computer function.
